definitions: To divert or modify (an instinctual impulse or a primitive form of behavior) into a culturally higher or more acceptable form.
To change directly from the solid to the gaseous state without becoming liquid.
To purify or refine a substance by heating it so that it changes into a gas and then condenses back into a solid.
examples: He learned to sublimate his aggressive impulses into competitive sports.
Dry ice will sublimate at room temperature.
The chemist will sublimate the compound to achieve greater purity.
